body.title: Librarian recall(X): FTS + vector + graph → RRF → synthesize
body.result: recall(X): FTS + vector cosine + graph walk + RRF merge + link following. Escalation: 1 Haiku call, writes back. 21 tests green.
body.description:
Implement the recall(X) endpoint — 'what do I know about X?' API for all agents. Inference-free in the common case.

Query path: (1) FTS on narrative + tags (free); (2) vector similarity via Scraps using pre-computed embeddings (cheap math, no inference); (3) graph walk from hits using typed interpretive_edges; (4) RRF merge of three result sets; (5) follow links (file path → read, URL → fetch) without inference; (6) optional inference escalation for nuance — if escalated, write result back so next recall is cheaper; (7) synthesize final answer on small pre-filtered set.

API: recall(query: str, limit: int = 10, escalate: bool = False) -> RecallResult. Separate MCP tool from research(X). Librarian starts before Igor in launcher so recall is always-on.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/ (recall endpoint, new MCP tool), UU launcher/superclaude (Librarian starts first)
**Design rules:** compiled-inference, igor-uses-not-contains; no inference in steps 1-5; inference only in escalation+synthesis; escalation must write back; recall distinct from research in MCP surface
**Scope boundary:** IN — recall(X) API, link following, escalation+write-back, always-on guarantee, launcher order; OUT — schema changes (T-memory-schema-v2), write path (T-memory-write-path), edge maintenance (T-librarian-edge-maintenance)
**Test plan:** recall('X') returns results; log shows no inference in FTS/vector/graph steps; second recall after escalation uses cached result; link following reads real file; Kill Igor → recall still works; recall and research are distinct MCP tools
